note_text: Maya is performing strongly in Python for Analytics and Database Fundamentals. She has asked for more advanced analytics project work and is a good candidate for peer tutoring.

Noah understands software design concepts but his Database Fundamentals project score has slipped. His advisor noted that commute issues affected March attendance and recommended a catch-up plan with weekly lab attendance.
Lina is a first-year student adjusting to the workload. She is motivated in Responsible AI Foundations but needs statistics practice and should attend the quantitative skills workshop.

Owen is at high academic risk. Advising notes mention missed labs late project work and a large unpaid fee balance. The intervention team recommended a meeting with the advisor financial aid and the Software Project Studio instructor.
Aisha is graduating soon and remains one of the strongest students in the cohort. She has no advising concerns and is preparing a capstone presentation on data quality.
Minh is steady in Python and Responsible AI but has borderline statistics performance. The advisor suggested study-group participation before the next assessment.
Emma has good attendance and solid grades. Her project team noted strong communication skills and she may be suitable for a student mentor role.
Carlos is at high risk in both Database Fundamentals and Python for Analytics. Notes mention irregular attendance overdue fees and difficulty completing programming labs without guided practice.
